title: Human-readable JSON payloads in API responses and requests
purpose: The describe endpoint returns payloads as raw byte arrays ([123, 34, 119, ...]). Starting a workflow requires pre-encoding input as {content_type, bytes}. Both are hostile to developers using curl or any HTTP client. The API should accept and return human-readable JSON where the content type is application/json.
task: Add JSON decoding/encoding at the HTTP/gRPC boundary: responses with content_type application/json decode the bytes to a JSON value before returning. Requests accept plain JSON objects as input (auto-wrapping in the Payload envelope). Preserve raw bytes for non-JSON content types.

requirements.title: Decode JSON payloads in describe and history responses
requirements.spec: WHEN a response includes a Payload with content_type application/json, THE SYSTEM SHALL decode the bytes field to a JSON value and return it as a structured object (e.g. {"content_type": "application/json", "data": {"name": "Ada"}}). For non-JSON content types, THE SYSTEM SHALL return the raw bytes as base64.
acceptance: Describe endpoint returns decoded JSON input/output fields, not byte arrays.
History events with JSON payloads show decoded data.
The content_type field is always present.

requirements.title: Accept plain JSON input for workflow start
requirements.spec: WHEN a start-workflow request includes an 'input' field that is a plain JSON object (not a Payload envelope), THE SYSTEM SHALL auto-wrap it as {content_type: 'application/json', bytes: <serialized>}. WHEN the input is already a Payload envelope (has content_type and bytes fields), THE SYSTEM SHALL use it as-is.
acceptance: curl with '"input": {"name": "Ada"}' starts the workflow successfully.
curl with the full envelope format still works (backwards compatible).
The error message for invalid input shows the expected format with an example.

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the internal Payload type in aion-core.
SHALL NOT change the gRPC wire format (proto stays as-is). This is HTTP-layer sugar only.
SHALL NOT decode payloads in the engine — only at the server boundary.
verification: curl start-workflow with plain JSON input succeeds.
curl describe returns human-readable JSON payloads.
